items.title: Fireworks light up the NT's skies on annual cracker night

children.content: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters busy putting out blazes.

gallery.altText: green and pink fireworks light up the sky over water with a crowd on the shore watching

thumbnail.altText: green and pink fireworks light up the sky over water with a crowd on the shore watching

items.title: Pair behind dramatic flood rescue awarded for going 'beyond the call of duty'

children.content: A helicopter pilot and a police officer who performed a daring flood rescue in Katherine earlier this year have been awarded for their actions as part of the Northern Territory's Territory Day awards.

image.altText: Man in high-vis sits on top of a 4WD vehicle half submerged in floodwater, holding a dog, as a helicopter hovers next to them.

items.title: NT power, water and sewerage prices increase by 5.3 per cent from today

children.content: The Northern Territory treasurer has defended an increase in power, water and sewerage prices, which are set to rise by 5.3 per cent from today.

image.altText: Power lines on a leafy green street.

items.title: Today in history: NT town of Katherine gazetted 100 years ago

children.content: Today marks 100 years of the NT town of Katherine, which has a rich history as a meeting place and crossroads to the north and as home to some of Australia's most stunning natural sites.

gallery.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo
A bronze statue of a man on a horse on a red concrete base
A bridge in the process of being built in 1924

thumbnail.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo
A bronze statue of a man on a horse on a red concrete base
A bridge in the process of being built in 1924

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a button-up shirt and serious facial expression.

items.title: 'Recurring issues' uncovered following surveillance of chopper sector

children.content: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

image.altText: A chopper shot from below, it is parked on green grass and a blue sky can be seen in the background.

items.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote NT

children.content: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

image.altText: a gate with a cut-out of a man on a horse silhouetted against a sunset.

items.title: Teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible

children.content: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

image.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

items.title: Study finds NT residents are the nation's happiest

children.content: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

image.altText: A woman, smiling, standing in a CBD mall.

items.title: Jillaroos, jackaroos and an iconic event: The Brunette Races in pictures

children.content: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

gallery.altText: A cowboy on top of a brown horse against a black night sky mid air as he is bucked from his horse.
High-angle shot of cowboy holding a saddle on grassy grounds, yellow.
A man in a large black cowboy hat pulls a saddle apart

items.title: Court finds staffer's relationship with 20yo parolee 'grossly inappropriate'

children.content: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A woman outside the court hides her face behind a cardigan, supporters around her attempt to block the view with umbrellas.

items.title: 'Kick in the guts': NT veterans join calls for health funding cap removal

children.content: The federal government wants to cap access to physical and mental health supports to $5,000 a year for injured war veterans, but there are growing concerns the move will push some into "a worse-off situation".

image.altText: A group of people sitting around a table in a cafe, an older man is pointing his finger as he speaks to the group.

images.altText: Young girl supporting the Americans cheers in the stands
Senegal players crowd the ref to complain about a penalty decision

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

children.content: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

image.altText: Harry Kane from behind holds out his hands in front of a crowd

children.content: Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2, while Harry Kane strikes twice after half-time to defeat DR Congo 2-1. Follow live.

updates.title: Team news: USA v Bosnia and Herzegovina

items.title: Government backs down on aged care tool as MPs stun at Midwinter Ball

children.content: The federal government has backed down on its aged care assessment tool to determine how much funding older Australians get. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese and Jodie Haydon walk to Midwinter Ball.

items.title: Influencers to be targeted in crackdown on gambling advertising

children.content: The Albanese government will target social media influencers and individuals who promote gambling on social media as part of its proposed laws restricting advertising.

image.altText: A woman with the parliament house flag in the background.

items.title: Before and after images show widespread impact of Venezuelan quakes

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

gallery.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

items.title: Extremists using 'codes' to evade detection online, royal commission hears

children.content: Far-right individuals adopt memes as a way to push "more palatable" antisemitism, which is less likely to be picked up by social media moderators.

image.altText: Hannah Rose in a blazer, with brunette hair, speaking before the commission.

items.title: Thousands of survivors of child sexual abuse face looming redress backlog

children.content: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

image.altText: Chris Coombes

items.title: Russian daredevil couple scale Empire State Building in proposal stunt

children.content: Two Russian daredevils, Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus, claim to be the duo who scaled the 102-storey, 443-metre tower for an apparent show of love and a marriage proposal.

gallery.altText: Two people in black clothing and masks standing atop a skyscraper antenna with a black protest banner in the wind.

thumbnail.altText: Two people in black clothing and masks standing atop a skyscraper antenna with a black protest banner in the wind.

items.title: US Navy crew member missing as chopper goes down in Arabian Sea

children.content: A US Navy service member is missing and three others are injured after their MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter went down in the Arabian Sea.

image.altText: A US Navy grey fighter helicopter hovering above the deck of an aircraft carrier, where three people in jackets are standing.

items.title: Djokovic apologises for pranking ball kid during 'vintage' Wimbledon win

children.content: A relaxed Novak Djokovic takes time out between sets of his second-round Wimbledon win over Stefanos Tsitsipas to play a prank on a ball kid.

image.altText: Novak Djokovic pranks a ball kid at Wimbledon.

items.title: Matthew Flinders's steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named  Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

gallery.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Jackass began with a gun shot; now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

image.altText: A bull gores a man dressed as a matador

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum first came from, but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

children.content: Queensland researchers are testing novel ways to kill varroa mites, which have developed a resistance to standard treatments.

items.title: XPeng electric vehicle owners still chasing $5k cashback months on

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

items.title: The US and UK have banned kids heading the ball. Should Australia follow?

items.title: Canberra water catchment shows signs of climate change

children.content: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.
